growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy supports innovation in clean technologies, enhancing sustainability and economic growth.
status_evidence: Programmes like EUDP are already in operation.
importance_rationale: The strategy represents a major overhaul of Denmark's approach to clean technology R&D.
description: Denmark is implementing a comprehensive strategy to support research and development in clean technologies, aiming to cut carbon emissions by 70% by 2030. This includes attracting private investment, enhancing public-private cooperation, and focusing on missions such as carbon capture, power-to-X, and eco-friendly agriculture. Major programmes like the Energy Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (EUDP) are central to this effort.
source_quote: The green research strategy is focused on four missions: 1) carbon capture, utilisation and storage, 2) power-to-X, 3) climate and eco-friendly agriculture and food production, 4) recycling and reduction of plastic waste.

growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentive encourages private sector investment in R&D, boosting innovation.
status_evidence: The tax allowance was applied in 2020 and extended.
importance_rationale: The temporary increase is a significant incentive for private R&D investment.
description: Denmark temporarily increased the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million in 2020 and 2021, later extended to 2022.
source_quote: The package also included a temporary increase in the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% in 2020 and 2021 (later extended to 2022) for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million.

growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance digital infrastructure and capabilities, supporting innovation and productivity.
importance_rationale: The strategy is a targeted initiative to improve digitalisation.
description: A new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022 based on the Digitization Partnership recommendations.
source_quote: A new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022 on the basis of the Digitization Partnership recommendations released in October 2021.

growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to reduce emissions and promote sustainable practices in agriculture and industry.
importance_rationale: The strategy is significant for its focus on sustainable practices and emissions reduction.
description: Denmark is investing in research and development to enhance agriculture's contribution to a net-zero target, focusing on alternatives to greenhouse gas intensive practices and improving emissions monitoring. The green research strategy also emphasizes developing green fuels and Power-to-X technologies.
source_quote: In the longer term, research and development can contribute to provide alternatives to GHG intensive practices and therefore abate emissions with limited leakage effects.

growth_orientation_rationale: The plan supports green innovation and technology scaling, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
importance_rationale: The plan provides significant funding for green R&D and innovation.
description: The Danish government announced a Green recovery plan, which includes substantial support for research and development of green technologies, with DKK 3,900 million allocated for this purpose. Additionally, a trans-partisan agreement allocated DKK 6 billion to the Danish Green Investment Fund to promote green innovation and scale up technologies in five key areas.
source_quote: A trans-partisan agreement between the Danish Government and other parties allocated DKK 6 billion to the Danish Green Investment Fund.

growth_orientation_rationale: Continued investment in wind energy supports renewable energy development and sustainability.
status_evidence: Ongoing funding and support for wind energy.
importance_rationale: The programme is a continuation of existing support for wind energy.
description: The Danish government has sustained support for wind research, development, and deployment, with significant subsidies and increasing funding throughout the 2000s, peaking at DKK 618 million in 2013.
source_quote: Sustained support for wind research, development and deployment, with significant subsidies in the late 1970s and 1980s and increasing funding throughout the 2000s, peaking at DKK 618 million in 2013.

growth_orientation_rationale: The development of wind energy islands and charging infrastructure supports renewable energy and sustainable transport.
importance_rationale: The plan involves large-scale infrastructure projects with significant investment.
description: The Danish government is planning to develop two wind energy islands to provide renewable electricity and sustainable fuels, with the first stage of the North Sea project estimated at EUR 29 billion. Additionally, a 10-year funding program for fast chargers on the trunk road network is part of the Infrastructure Plan 2035.
source_quote: The Danish government is planning to develop two wind energy islands, an artificial island in the North Sea and the Baltic island of Bornholm, to provide renewable electricity, sustainable fuels and interconnection with other countries.
